title: Reduce polling log noise and optimize refresh
description: Remove verbose SETTINGS_TAB log messages during polling and refresh. Keep error logging. Remove per-file-node insertion logging. Only log on actual manifest changes, not every poll cycle check.

title: Wire #[Mark:] → todos.json chain → Expected Diff in check_completion
description:
FILE: tabs/ag_forge_tab/modules/quick_clip/tabs/planner_tab.py — extend check_completion()
FILE: plans/debug_todo_analyzer.py — PlanManager.scan_code_marks()
CHAIN: scan_code_marks() finds #[Mark:ID] in .py files → cross-ref todos.json by task ID → surface in completion window with: mark location, linked task title/status, whether task has an expected diff entry
STRUCTURE: Each mark entry shows: filename:line | #[Mark:ID] | → task_ID: title (STATUS)
EXPECTED DIFF: todos.json tasks with a "diff_ref" field or a matching entry in plans/Diffs/ → shown as "Expected: yes/no" per mark
ALSO: populate Refs dir button count with #[Mark:] count found
DOC REF: plans/Phase_20-23_Integration_Map.md section 23.1 (interlink gaps research)
TARGET CLASS: PlannerSuite.check_completion() — append section after runtime_bugs block
WHEREIN context: "PlannerSuite::check_completion" already used in existing code pattern

title: Fix missing context data in enriched_changes (classes, methods, before_after)
description: The live watcher computes context, before_after values, but stores incomplete data in enriched_changes. Need to add: classes (from diff), methods (from diff), before_after_values list, additions/deletions/net_change stats. These are computed but never stored in the manifest dict at lines 313-326 of interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py.

title: Right-click undo with tab refresh and test cycle
description: Setup right-click → undo + refresh on selected tab. Create in undo_changes.py a test/undo/refresh cycle: (1) [Test] button swaps running tab with pending_changes version, (2) Mark change as Unstable/Stable in pool, (3) [Save]/[Undo] to commit or revert. Hook runtime exec context + traceback back into logger_util for attribution pool. Enables testing changes on the spot.

title: Execution verification layer: probe live tab instances for callable methods
description: After tab init, probe the live instance via getattr() to verify: (1) key methods listed in AST profile actually exist on the instance, (2) bound tkinter callbacks (button commands) are callable, (3) sub-notebook tabs have visible non-empty children. Store results as 'execution_probe' in tab_init_results. Methods present in profile but missing from instance = MISSING_METHOD blame event. Blank widget children = BLANK_PANEL event. This closes the loop: change event → code changed → method gone → probe finds it missing → blame attributed.

title: Port TodoSync from Os_Toolkit → debug_todo_analyzer.py
description:
SOURCE: tabs/action_panel_tab/Os_Toolkit.py — classes TodoSync, PlanManager
KEY METHODS TO PORT: PlanManager.scan_code_marks() scans #[Mark:ID-STATUS] in .py files; TodoSync.sync() three-way ~/.claude/tasks/ ↔ plans/todos.json ↔ marks; TodoSync._find_latest_claude_session()
TARGET FILE: plans/debug_todo_analyzer.py
PATH SWAP: replace all Os_Toolkit path constants with trainer_paths.TRAINER_PATHS — import from tabs/action_panel_tab/trainer_paths.py
trainer_paths keys: todos=plans/todos.json, plans=plans/, claude_tasks=~/.claude/tasks/, runtime_bugs=plans/runtime_bugs.json
SCAN ROOT for marks: DATA_DIR (Trainer/Data/) excluding backup/ __pycache__ tabs/action_panel_tab/
ENTRY POINT: def sync_all() → returns dict {claude_synced: int, marks_found: int, todos_updated: int}
ALSO PORT: ConformityAction class for check_integrate_conformers() (task_26_3)
NOTE: Os_Toolkit uses zenity for prompts — replace with logger_util.log_message() for all output

title: Verify attribute flow from Os_Toolkit to Planner Board
description:
Critical Issue: The Planner Board shows empty/unwired attributes, indicating data isn't flowing from Os_Toolkit.

This suggests either:
1. Os_Toolkit is returning None/Empty data due to read failures
2. planner_tab.py is failing to parse Os_Toolkit output
3. The task properties in the Task Tree aren't being populated with session data

After fixing the backup reader, manually seed Os_Toolkit database with key file scans so there's actual data to display in Planner Board.

Then trace the exact path: Os_Toolkit output -> planner_tab.py -> Task Tree properties.

title: Make Latest Changes tree fully dynamic (remove height limit, newest first)
description: Remove height=10 hard limit on changes_tree. Sort files with most recent changes first (not alphabetical). Expand file nodes with events. Update status label with count. Ensure all files appear regardless of count.

title: Phase 5.11: Dynamic Context Panel Renaming - "Backup Details" → "Change Context"
description:
Rename and repurpose lower panel to show dynamic context based on selection.

Current: "Backup Details" - static display
New: "Change Context" - dynamic based on what user views

Display modes (auto-switching):
1. When event selected: Show Before/After tokens (Phase 5.9)
2. When file profile shown: Show File Profile Tree (Phase 5.8)
3. When right-click profile: Show deep code structure
4. When AoE hovered: Show dependent functions
5. When blame button clicked: Show blame analysis (Phase 5.6)

Header changes dynamically:
- "📊 Change Context: Before/After States"
- "🏛️ File Profile: settings_tab.py"
- "🔗 Dependencies: Affected by this change"
- "⚠️ Risk Analysis: Why this is risky"

Target: Refactor lower panel in settings_tab.py to support multiple modes
Keep consistent framing/styling across all modes

title: Phase 5.8: File Profile Tree Viewer - Manifest-Driven Structure
description:
Create file profile tree viewer showing code structure from manifest data.

Display hierarchy:
📄 File: settings_tab.py
├── 📦 Imports
│   ├── tkinter as tk
│   ├── tkinter.ttk
│   └── json
├── 🏛️ Classes
│   ├── SettingsTab
│   │   ├── 🔧 __init__()
│   │   ├── 🔧 create_ui()
│   │   └── 🔧 create_action_panel()
│   └── [ClassName2]
├── ⚙️ Functions
│   ├── helper_function_1()
│   └── helper_function_2()
└── 🏷️ Marks/#[Mark:XXX]
    ├── #[Mark:PHASE_5_4_ACTION_PANEL]
    └── #[Mark:ENRICHED_CHANGE_LOG_OUTPUT]

Features:
- Pull data from manifest.json profile section
- Expandable/collapsible tree nodes
- Show method signatures and decorators
- Highlight methods touched by recent changes
- Color-code by change status (added=green, modified=yellow, removed=red)
- Click to show line numbers and source context

Target: New "File Profile" panel in lower context area
Data source: version_manifest['enriched_changes'][event]['file'] → manifest[file]['profile']

title: Connect Tab Manager blame display to enriched_changes and execution probe
description: Tab Manager currently shows ☢️ for damaged versions based on file path in blame list. Needs: (1) cross-reference against enriched_changes to show which specific EVENTS changed this file, (2) cross-reference against execution_probe to show which methods are currently MISSING or FAILING, (3) colour-code: orange=changed-and-unverified, red=changed-and-failing, green=changed-and-verified. The Tab Manager already has the tree structure - it just needs the enriched_changes and probe data wired in.

title: Phase 5.4: Design & Implement Bottom Action Panel UI
description:
Create new "Action Panel" in Backups & Logs tab (settings_tab.py) with:
- Left: Latest Changes List (shows File | Event# | Verb | Feature, color-coded by risk)
- Right: Action Buttons (Undo Selected Change, View Blame, Show Risk Assessment)
- Coordination: When file selected in tree, bottom list shows its latest changes
- Risk colors propagate from changes to tree nodes

Panel height: ~150-200px, vertically stout
Location: Bottom of Backups & Logs tab in Data/tabs/settings_tab/settings_tab.py

title: Add Trainer folder tree + filesystem root to File Explorer tab
description: In DigitalBiosphereApp.setup_explorer_tab() / _build_explorer_content(), add two root sections to the file explorer treeview: 1) /Trainer (pre-expanded, shows /Trainer/Data/ subtree as known project root), 2) / (filesystem root — lazy expand). Currently only loads Path.home() on init. Add a sidebar or top-section with these as pinned locations. Also coordinate with Context tree: clicking a file in Explorer should select its node in Context tree if present.

title: Wire filesync.py temporal project inference to Active Project tracking
description:
Critical Gap: Temporal inference not coordinated with task/plan system

filesync.py has temporal project inference that clusters files into projects. This needs to:

1. **Feed to Planner Board** as "Active Project" property
2. **Link to task_context.json** - set active_project field
3. **Enable Checklist marking** - show files that belong to active project
4. **Plan correlation** - show which plans reference active project files

Flow:
1. temporal_inference detects "Core System" project cluster
2. Os_Toolkit.latest includes "Active project: Core System | 5 files | 3 tasks"
3. planner_tab shows all related tasks grouped under active project
4. Checklist shows files belonging to active project with marked/unmarked status
5. task_context.json auto-populates active_project field

Requires:
- filesync.py output integration into version_manifest
- Planner Board "Active Project" signal header
- Checklist UI project grouping

title: Copy Babel_v01a → tabs/action_panel_tab/ and wire path constants
description: Copy Babel_v01a to Trainer as tabs/action_panel_tab/, excluding the large exports dir and pycache. Then update path constants so todo sync points at Trainer's plans/todos.json and DATA_DIR.

title: Project manifest viewer prep — Project_Template_001 format in planner
description:
DEFERRED but prepped: research pass only for now.
DOC: Find Project_Template_001.md / similar in plans/ or action_panel_tab/plans/ — read its schema
TARGET: plans/interlink_gaps.md (task_23_1) — cross-ref all .md files vs todos.json
SCHEMA TO FIND: project_id, linked_plans, linked_todos, linked_ag_forge {epic, plan}, expected_diffs, mark_refs
VIEWER LOCATION: new right_notebook tab in planner_tab "📋 Project" — shows project_manifest.json for the active base_path project
CREATION UX: [+ New Project] button → form dialog (project_id, description, link to epic dir) → writes project_manifest.json to base_path root
SEARCH: grep plans/ for "project_id\|linked_plans\|manifest" to find existing template format
ACTION: read plans/ dir, find any *manifest* or *template* file, read structure, add RESEARCH task with findings to todos.json phase_23
NOTE: This feeds the "per project top-level manifest" the user mentioned — do research pass first before any UI build

title: Fix Unstable version marking (orange color) in backup tree
description: System versions listed in backup tree should show 'Unstable' with orange coloring. Previous agent attempted this but implementation missed - code didn't change. Check settings_tab.py backup tree population for version status marking logic.

title: Integrate Blame and Risk into undo popup (remove basic messagebox popups)
description: Replace on_view_blame() and on_show_risk() messagebox.showinfo calls with tabs in the existing UndoChangesDialog. Add a "Blame & Risk" tab to undo_changes.py that shows blame analysis and risk assessment together.

title: Plans dir selector + path indicator in planner_tab
description:
Add a path bar above the PanedWindow in PlannerSuite.setup_ui(). 
FILE: tabs/ag_forge_tab/modules/quick_clip/tabs/planner_tab.py
INSERT: before `main_container = tk.PanedWindow(...)` around line 72 (after setup_ui def)
WIDGETS: tk.Frame(self) path_bar → tk.Label showing self.base_path (self.path_label) + tk.Button "📂 Set Dir" → filedialog.askdirectory() → update self.base_path → call setup_directories() + load_directory_structure() + update self.path_label
INDICATOR: Label text = f"📁 {self.base_path}" truncated to last 60 chars with "..." prefix if long
TARGET CLASS: PlannerSuite.setup_ui() — insert path_bar frame before main_container pack
ALSO: save chosen path to config.json at base_path root so it persists across sessions

title: Add Debug Config tab to undo_changes.py
description: Add a 5th tab to UndoChangesDialog: 'Debug Config'. Shows: (1) Risk weight config - what rules fired, their individual scores, confidence calculation breakdown. (2) Attribution intelligence level - count of populated vs empty fields, data richness score. (3) Tab init registry data for the file's owning tab - source_file, widget_summary, status. (4) Event pool links - other events on same file, cross-references. This replaces the idea of separate debug windows.

title: Map PyManifest ClassInfo/ImportInfo onto Biosphere 3D nodes
description: Use ManifestEngine or per-file StaticAnalyzer to feed the 3D biosphere visualization: Classes → ENTITY layer nodes colored by verb_category mix of their methods; Imports → CONNECTION edges between files (import graph); FunctionInfo.complexity → node size. This replaces/augments the current stub EntityLayer. Add a new view_mode='codebase' to the view mode radio buttons. Data sourced from StaticAnalyzer run on /Trainer/Data/*.py files.

title: Phase 5.9: Before/After State Visualization - Token-Level Changes
description:
Show before/after code state with token-level highlighting for changed values.

Features:
- When event selected, display side-by-side before/after code snippets
- Highlight changed tokens (variable names, values, keywords)
- Show line-by-line diff with context
- Color scheme:
  - 🔴 Red: removed tokens
  - 🟢 Green: added tokens
  - 🟡 Yellow: modified tokens
- Show what changed: class name, method signature, variable assignments, etc.
- Track "tokens involved" as mentioned for accountability

Example visualization:
```
Before:                          After:
def old_method():               def new_method(x, y):
    print('old')                    result = x + y
                                    return result
```

Tokens tracked: function name changed, parameters added, return added

Target: Dynamic context panel below profile tree
Data source: logger_util.extract_before_after_values() + enhanced diff parsing

title: Expected Attribution pool — live watcher class-change warning + todo append
description:
CONCEPT: when live watcher detects a change in a .py file, run StaticAnalyzer on the diff to check: did any class get new methods but NO corresponding signature changes in key logger calls or property additions inside __init__ / call-chains? If so → warn + auto-append todo.
FILES TO MODIFY: interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py live watcher section (around where log_change_attribution is called), logger_util.py, plans/debug_todo_analyzer.py
DETECTION LOGIC: 
  - extract_methods_from_diff() already exists in logger_util.py:line 161
  - extract_context_function_and_class() already exists in logger_util.py:line 217
  - if new method detected AND no log_ux_event/log_message call added in same diff → flag as "unlogged change"
  - if class change detected AND no #[Mark:] annotation added → flag as "unmarked change"
WARN OUTPUT: log_message(f"{warn} ATTR: {file} — new method {method} has no logger call or Mark annotation")
  → triggers _auto_create_warn_task() → runtime_bugs.json INTEGRATE type
TODO APPEND: if matching task ID found in todos.json for that file → append note to task description: "Live watcher detected: {method} added {timestamp} — no attribution signal"
DOC REF: plans/Phase_24-26_Babel_Grep_Integration.md section 26 (completion conformers)
6W1H POOL: enriched_changes already has verb/stats/classes/methods/wherein — feed these as "expected attribution" fields when creating the warn task
TARGET: log_change_attribution() in logger_util.py:line 241 — add post-extraction check block

title: Organize right-side file tree with sub-node nesting and per-node toggling
description: Right side file tree needs: (1) sub-node nesting separating backups vs running files, (2) per-node toggling to reduce launch load, (3) leave live changes for improved resource availability. Wrap execution in per-node toggling rather than loading everything at once.

title: Fix key mismatches in undo popup specs tab
description: Popup reads wrong keys: (1) 'confidence' should be 'risk_confidence', (2) 'reasons' should be 'risk_reasons', (3) 'feature_id' not stored - should show 'feature', (4) context_class stored but 'classes' list empty - should fall back to context_class if classes list empty. All stored in enriched_changes but popup reads wrong field names.

title: Tkinter silent failure hooks: wrap all button/callback bindings in base_tab
description: Tkinter swallows errors in callbacks silently. Base_tab needs: (1) a register_callback(widget, command, feature_name) helper that wraps the command in try/except before binding, (2) auto-discovery of all Button/Checkbutton/Menu widgets in the tab's frame after init, binding them to error-catching wrappers. When a callback fails it fires: mark_feature_failed(feature_name, error) + log with full traceback. This surfaces silent runtime failures that currently disappear into the void.

title: Phase 5.7: Right-Click Profile Integration - Action Panel Context Menu
description:
Implement right-click context menu on action panel change listings to "Show Profile". 

Features:
- Right-click on any event entry (e.g., "#[Event:0020] | settings_tab.py | add | Settings Tab")
- Show context menu with "📊 View File Profile" option
- Clicking opens file profile in lower context panel
- Profile shows manifest data: imports, classes, methods, decorators
- Before/after state visualization with tokens highlighted
- Integration with file tree on right side

Integration points:
- Query manifest for file's profile data
- Load imports → classes → methods hierarchy
- Show state changes (what tokens changed in before/after)
- Allow navigation back to full file tree

Target file: Data/tabs/settings_tab/settings_tab.py
Integration: create_action_panel() method + new on_show_file_profile()

title: Add attribution intelligence scoring to risk assessment
description: Risk assessment should track data richness: how many attribution fields are populated vs null/empty. Score = populated_fields / total_fields. Low intelligence (few fields) → lower confidence, note 'limited attribution data'. High intelligence → can increase confidence. Store as 'attribution_score' and 'attribution_fields_count' in enriched_changes. Show in Debug Config tab.

title: Fix Digital Biosphere 3D rendering layout bugs
description: Three confirmed layout bugs in DigitalBiosphereVisualizer: 1) Matplotlib canvas is created inside setup_left_panel() (wrong parent — lands in 300px left panel), should be in setup_visualization_panel(). 2) Canvas widget is .pack()ed twice (lines 858 and 868), causing layout conflicts. 3) setup_visualization_panel() is a stub showing only "Visualization Panel Ready" text. Fix: move canvas creation to setup_visualization_panel(), remove double pack, fix pack order for bottom_panel (must be packed before left/right). Also remove redundant main_container.pack() call in DigitalBiosphereApp.setup_visualization_tab().

title: Phase 5.10: AoE (Area of Effect) Pooling - Cascading Impact Visualization
description:
Show cascading impact of changes - which files/functions depend on changed code.

Features:
- Use profile_util call graph data to show dependents
- Build "AoE pool" - list of files/functions affected by this change
- Color-code changes in latest changes list by AoE size:
  - Small AoE (1-2 dependents) = Green
  - Medium AoE (3-5 dependents) = Yellow  
  - Large AoE (5+ dependents) = Red/Orange
- Show in tooltip when hovering over event in action panel
- Integrate with risk scoring: CRITICAL file + Large AoE = extra warning
- Store in version_manifest['event_pools'] and cross-reference

Example:
"Event #0020 affects 7 functions: 
  - Module A.ClassX.method_1()
  - Module B.function_Z()
  - Module C.ClassY.method_3()
  ... (4 more)"

Target: Enhance event registration in recovery_util.register_event()
Display: Tooltip + color indicator in action panel + detailed popup

title: Sync Todos button in planner bottom panel + dir button count update
description:
FILE: tabs/ag_forge_tab/modules/quick_clip/tabs/planner_tab.py
ADD TO: watcher_frame (the frame at bottom containing Task Watcher checkbox + Check Completion + Generate Report)
INSERT: tk.Button "🔄 Sync Todos" command=self.sync_todos
NEW METHOD sync_todos():
  1. Call plans/debug_todo_analyzer.py sync_all() via importlib (once ported, task_44)
  2. Fallback: subprocess.run(['python3', trainer_paths.TRAINER_PATHS['os_toolkit'], 'todo', 'view'], capture_output=True) → log stdout via _trainer_log
  3. After sync, call _refresh_dir_button_counts() to update Structure tab dir buttons with live counts from todos.json
  4. Update status_bar with result summary
_refresh_dir_button_counts(): reads todos.json → counts tasks per phase group → maps to dir labels:
  Epics → top-level projects, Plans → phase count, Tasks → READY task count, Refs → #[Mark:] count from runtime_bugs.json
NAMING: follows existing button convention (tk.Button, pack side=tk.LEFT, padx=5)
TARGET STRING in file: "tk.Button(watcher_frame, text=\"Check Completion\""

title: Fix critical bugs from latest_bugs.md
description: 1. Line 2: `name 'recovery_util' is not defined` - missing import in interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py around line 607 where runtime error tracking stores to manifest. 2. Lines 37-49: JSON decode error - unterminated string in version_manifest at line 2404. Likely from storing large file content with unescaped quotes in change_states. Need JSON escaping or binary encoding for before/after content. See /plans/latest_bugs.md for full context.

title: Phase 5.13: System Blueprint Tab Integration - "Show Profile" Coordinated Browsing
description:
Integrate System Blueprints tab with new profile system for coordinated browsing.

Current state: System Blueprints is placeholder for file tree + eventual call graph

Enhancement vision:
- "Show Profile" from action panel OR file tree opens profile in System Blueprints
- File tree on right side shows entire codebase structure
- Right-click on file tree nodes: "Show Recent Changes", "Show Profile", "Show Dependents"
- Call graph integration: show which functions call this function
- Optional try/except import for digital_biosphere integration
- When digital_biosphere available: use its call graph data
- Fallback to profile_util if digital_biosphere not available

Coordinated features:
- Clicking file in action panel highlights it in file tree
- Expanding a class shows all methods with their recent changes
- Hovering method shows AoE pool
- Color coding: risk colors + change status colors + AoE colors

Target: Extend System Blueprints tab to support profile viewer
Integration: Link action panel → file tree → profile system

title: Integrate PyManifest StaticAnalyzer into Context tree
description: Use StaticAnalyzer from pymanifest/py_manifest_augmented.py to add imports/classes/methods/verbs branches per file node in refresh_context_tree(). StaticAnalyzer is a pure AST tool, no external deps. Branches to add: 📦 Imports (N) → each import with module/line, 🏛 Classes (N) → each class with bases/method count, methods listed as children with verb_category color, 🔗 Dependencies (N local, N external). Run on-demand when file node is expanded.

title: BUG-001: Fix recovery_util import
description: Add `import recovery_util` to interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py around line 607. Was missing, causing NameError on launch.

title: BUG-002: Fix JSON corruption from change_states
description: In interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py live watcher: store before/after content to backup/change_states/{event_id}_before.txt + _after.txt. Store only file paths in version_manifest.json. Update tabs/settings_tab/undo_changes.py to read from file paths.

title: Make Blame and Risk buttons open to their respective tabs in undo popup
description: Currently both buttons open the same dialog to the default tab. Blame button should auto-select the Blame & Risk tab, Risk button same. Pass a tab_index or tab_name parameter to UndoChangesDialog to auto-focus the relevant tab on open.

title: Surface widget_profile and tab context to enriched_changes
description: When the live watcher fires a change event for a file, cross-reference TAB_REGISTRY to find which tab owns that file (via source_file match). Store tab_name, widget_summary, and tab_status in enriched_changes for that event. This connects the change event to the owning tab's widget tree, enabling AoE: 'this change to settings_tab.py affects a tab with 3 Treeviews and 2 Notebooks'.

title: Phase 5.3: Implement Risk Detection Algorithm
description:
Analyze changes to flag potentially breaking modifications. Create recovery_util.assess_change_risk(file_path, diff_text, feature_id) with rules for: CRITICAL files changed, removed imports/functions, large refactors, cascading impact detection.

Output: risk_level (LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH|CRITICAL), confidence score, list of triggered rules, suggested_undo, cascading_impact_estimate

Risk rules: (1) CRITICAL file modified (logger_util, recovery_util, interactive_trainer) = HIGH+CRITICAL, (2) Verb='remove' + (import|def) = MEDIUM-HIGH, (3) Verb='refactor' + >50 lines = MEDIUM, (4) Docstring not updated = LOW, (5) Function with 3+ callers = MEDIUM cascade

title: Add {wherein} to logger_util and Changes treeview
description: Add optional wherein=None param to log_ux_event() in logger_util.py (shows in live log file output). Add 'wherein' column to Changes treeview in settings_tab.py create_action_panel section.

title: Phase 5.12: UX/UI Design Refinement - Button/Listing Visual Hierarchy
description:
Refine visual design now that baseline is established. Focus on:

Button Design:
- Consider icon + text labeling consistency
- Hover/disabled states clear
- Action buttons should have clear visual priority
- Consider grouping related actions

Listing/Event Display:
- Risk symbol (🔴🟠🟡🟢) + text redundancy - pick best
- Event ID formatting: #[Event:0001] - consider abbreviating to E-0001 for compact display
- Color coding consistent across all views (risk colors should match system-wide)
- Sortable columns? Filtering by risk level?
- Hover reveals AoE info

Typography & Spacing:
- Monospace font for file names/code? Or mixed?
- Line height in listbox for readability
- Padding/margins for visual breathing room
- Dark theme consistency (currently #1e1e1e, #2d2d2d, #d4d4d4)

Accessibility:
- Keyboard navigation of action panel and listings
- Screen reader friendly labels
- High contrast for all text
- Consistent focus indicators

Target: Create UX/UI style guide document capturing decisions
Files to refine: settings_tab.py (action panel + context panel)

title: Round-trip execution chain: trigger → exec → result → attribution pool
description: The full loop: (1) Change event detected on file X, (2) method M in file X was modified, (3) on next launch, probe finds M exists on instance, (4) on first call to M, result (success/traceback) is captured, (5) result fed back to enriched_changes for event that modified M as 'execution_verified: true/false'. This connects change attribution to actual runtime outcome. The on_backup_select case: change was detected, but we never verified the method executed successfully - that gap is exactly what this closes.

title: Add [Sync Todos] button to planner_tab bottom panel
description: Target: tabs/ag_forge_tab/modules/quick_clip/tabs/planner_tab.py bottom panel (watcher_frame area, lines ~241-254). Add [🔄 Sync Todos] button that calls debug_todo_analyzer.py sync logic (plans/debug_todo_analyzer.py). Three-way sync: ~/.claude/tasks/ ↔ plans/todos.json ↔ #[Mark:] annotations. Write result to plans/Refs/latest_sync.json. Show result count in status_bar. Task ref: task_25_1 / task_45.

title: Audit Tab Manager ↔ Backup & Logs context coordination
description: Tab Manager has a file tree and receives context. Check for coordination issues or missing context between Tab Manager and Backup & Logs sub-tabs. Both are tk.Notebook tabs. Should pass context about Tab children (panes/widgets/functions) to support blame inference for non-functional tabs based on execution events vs changes.

title: Add Latest Report sub-tab to planner right_notebook
description: Target: tabs/ag_forge_tab/modules/quick_clip/tabs/planner_tab.py. Add 📊 Latest Report tab to the right_notebook (the tab panel on the right side). Runs: subprocess Os_Toolkit.py latest (no -z flag) → stdout → ScrolledText display. [🔄 Refresh] button triggers re-run. Called automatically on Generate Report. Task ref: task_25_2.

title: Update todos.json with current phase progress
description: todos.json updated with Phases 7-10: PyManifest relocation, Debug Profile training, Attribution config, Biosphere codebase layer. Phase 5.9 UNBLOCKED (py_manifest_augmented.py IS the CLI system). Files moved to map_tab/ with correct backup/history/ paths.

title: Connect PyManifest FunctionInfo.verb_category + call graph to attribution
description: Three attribution enrichments from pymanifest: 1) verb_category (CREATE/READ/UPDATE/DELETE/VALIDATE/CONVERT/CALCULATE) maps to our verb field in enriched_changes — run StaticAnalyzer on modified file, find changed functions, pull their verb_category. 2) FunctionInfo.calls list enables AoE (Area of Effect) — which other methods call the changed function. 3) ClassInfo.bases enables inheritance risk propagation. Feed into enriched_changes risk_reasons. This connects Task #25 (nested class AoE) and Task #10 (AoE pooling).

title: Create task_context.json pooling system for richcontextual metadata
description:
Critical Infrastructure: task###_context.json files

Each task needs a persistent context file that pools data from all systems:

Structure needed:
```json
{
  "task_id": "task_19_1",
  "title": "BUG-001: Fix recovery_util import",
  "wherein": "interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py",
  "active_project": "Core System Fixes",
  "status": "READY",
  
  "attribution": {
    "events": ["#[Event:0019]", "#[Event:0016]", "#[Event:0020]"],
    "recent_diffs": [...],
    "risk_summary": "CRITICAL"
  },
  
  "code_profile": {
    "file": "interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py",
    "classes": [...],
    "methods_changed": ["__init__", "run"],
    "complexity": 5,
    "imports_added": ["recovery_util"]
  },
  
  "temporal_context": {
    "cluster_id": "...",
    "peer_files": [...],
    "time_window": "24h",
    "activity_log": [...]
  },
  
  "expected_diffs": {
    "expected_files_to_change": [...],
    "expected_methods": [...],
    "verification_points": [...]
  }
}
```

This file is generated by:
1. Os_Toolkit.py query at task focus
2. Planner UI right-click context menu  
3. Pooling: enriched_changes + py_manifest + temporal inference + UX logs

Used by:
1. Checklist UI (shows what's marked vs unmarked)
2. Undo dialog (shows full context before undoing)
3. Task navigation (breadcrumbs with ref links)
4. Expected diffs comparison

title: Add classify_tab_domain, UX_EVENT_LOG, log_ux_event to logger_util.py
description: Add TAX domain classification and UX event log to logger_util.py. See plan for exact code.

title: Add import detection to enriched change attribution
description: When a diff adds/removes an import statement, enriched_changes should explicitly flag it (e.g. 'imports_added': ['datetime'], 'imports_removed': []). This feeds into risk assessment AND profile evolution tracking. Currently the watcher computes verb/classes/methods but doesn't extract import changes from diffs. Need a new extract_imports_from_diff() in logger_util.py and store results in enriched_changes.

title: Parse nested class properties for AoE attribution
description: Class context showing N/A in undo UI. Need to: (1) Extract parent class from diff context, (2) Identify nested properties/methods within class, (3) Store in enriched_changes for AoE (Area of Effect) blame - which OTHER classes/methods depend on this one? Maps to existing AoE pooling todos (#10). This enables cascading impact inference.

title: Fix backup reader: handle history directory structure correctly
description:
Critical Issue: Os_Toolkit is crashing with [Errno 21] Is a directory when trying to read backup history.

Root cause: The history/ directory structure stores files as directories (one dir per original file path), with timestamped backups inside each directory. Os_Toolkit code is trying to open() these directory names as files.

The action_verify_backups() function at line 1877 uses rglob and checks is_file() which should be correct, but there may be other code paths that don't check properly, or the action_backup() function is looking in the wrong location.

Files to check:
- Os_Toolkit.py action_backup() at line 1746 (looking for files at wrong path)
- Os_Toolkit.py action_audit_workflow() at line 1928 (reads from pending_changes which may have invalid paths)
- Any code that directly opens files from history without checking is_dir()

Expected outcome: Os_Toolkit reads all history files correctly and counts backups accurately.

title: Add [Consolidate Plans] button + {wherein} display to planner
description: Target: tabs/ag_forge_tab/modules/quick_clip/tabs/planner_tab.py. Add [📦 Consolidate Plans] button that calls PlanConsolidator.discover_scattered_plans() from tabs/action_panel_tab/babel_data/inventory/core/plan_consolidator.py. Shows confirmation dialog listing found .md files outside plans/. Also: show {wherein} field from todos.json entries in the task viewer. Task ref: task_43, task_48.
